content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from Topic A's ICM/RND curious agent research) can be repurposed as a meta-learning objective to prioritize which new experiences get consolidated into long-term agent memory, rather than using fixed heuristic or task-specific rules for memory pruning. This creates a self-organizing memory consolidation system where an agent automatically prioritizes consolidating surprising, generalizable knowledge that improves future few-shot adaptation.
